What’s the difference between hair loss and hair shedding?

Hair shedding and hair loss are often confused with one another, but it’s important to know the difference. Hair shedding refers to hair increasingly falling from the scalp, usually as part of a temporary disruption in the hair growth cycle, says Dr. Alexandra Bowles, a board-certified dermatologist at MONA Dermatology. Hair loss, on the other hand, happens when hair follicles stop producing new hairs or gradually shrink over time, she says.

The key difference: When hair sheds, the follicle is still healthy and capable of growing back, while those experiencing hair loss may have follicle damage or thinning. “Shedding typically feels sudden, while hair loss often appears more gradual and patterned,” says Bowles.
What causes hair loss versus shedding?

There are a variety of factors that can contribute to hair loss, including hormonal imbalances, genetics, age, autoimmune disorders and certain medical conditions, says board-certified cosmetic dermatologist Dr. Michele Green.

Androgen hormones (which are male sex hormones found in both men and women that play a key role in reproductive health and body development), such as dihydrotestosterone (DHT), usually play a significant role in hair loss. “Some people may be sensitive to DHT or genetically predisposed to higher levels of DHT and other androgen hormones,” says Green. Women often experience hair loss as a result of lowered estrogen levels, especially after pregnancy, during menopause and through changes in birth control.

Autoimmune disorders such as alopecia areata, which is when the immune system attacks its own hair follicles, as well as certain medical conditions like hypothyroidism, can cause sudden patchy hair loss or thinning.

Excessive hair shedding (called telogen effluvium in the medical world) can be triggered by physical or emotional stressors, including surgery, childbirth, infections, chronic stress, death of a loved one or divorce. “Excessive shedding typically begins two months after a stressor and often resolves on its own several months after,” says Green.
Diet also plays a key role: crash diets or rapid weight loss can trigger excessive hair shedding, and nutritional deficiencies in protein and iron can lead to hair loss and thinning.
How can you tell if you’re experiencing hair loss or shedding?
Below are a few key factors that make it easier to tell whether you’re experiencing hair loss or shedding.

Hair shedding: “Normal hair shedding can look like several strands falling out while brushing or washing, typically up to 150 strands a day,” says Green. With excessive hair shedding, you’ll usually notice this happens rapidly over a short period of time. People will usually notice hair shedding three to six months after the trigger, says Dr. Nkem Ugonabo, a board-certified dermatologist at UnionDerm in New York City.Hair loss: You’ll often notice a receding hairline, a widening part, thinning at the temples or crown, bald spots or a more exposed scalp, says Green. For many people, it takes months or even years to notice hair loss, depending on the severity.

Hair shedding: “Normal hair shedding can look like several strands falling out while brushing or washing, typically up to 150 strands a day,” says Green. With excessive hair shedding, you’ll usually notice this happens rapidly over a short period of time. People will usually notice hair shedding three to six months after the trigger, says Dr. Nkem Ugonabo, a board-certified dermatologist at UnionDerm in New York City.

Hair loss: You’ll often notice a receding hairline, a widening part, thinning at the temples or crown, bald spots or a more exposed scalp, says Green. For many people, it takes months or even years to notice hair loss, depending on the severity.
How do you treat hair loss versus shedding?

Though they are very different, many treatments for hair loss and hair shedding overlap since both support scalp health and follicle function, says Bowles. However, shedding often improves with time and supportive care, whereas hair loss typically requires ongoing treatment to slow progression. Here’s what experts suggest for each one:

Propecia (finasteride) is an FDA-approved oral medication prescribed by a doctor that prevents testosterone from turning into dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which helps treat hair loss in men. It’s not yet FDA-approved for women, though it’s commonly prescribed off-label to women experiencing hair loss who aren’t planning to become pregnant, says Green. Dutasteride is another DHT-blocking prescription oral medication that helps increase hair density and growth, but it is not yet FDA-approved for either men or women (but still prescribed off-label to men), says Green.Topical minoxidil, often known as its brand name Rogaine, is an FDA-approved treatment that strengthens hair follicles and promotes hair growth and retention. Dermatologists commonly prescribe oral minoxidil for both hair loss and shedding, says Green. However, you’ll need to use it consistently, or else the new hair you grew will shed again, experts say.Ketoconazole is a common ingredient in dandruff shampoos, but paired with a hair loss treatment like minoxidil, it can potentially increase hair growth, says Ugonabo.Spironolactone is an oral antiandrogen medication prescribed to women experiencing hair loss. It regulates androgen (male hormone) production to promote hair growth, says Green. It’s also effective for women experiencing hair loss due to Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS), she says.Platelet-rich plasma (PRP) injections, which involves taking a sample of blood and spinning it to separate the growth factors and proteins that promote tissue regeneration and hair follicle healing, says Green. It’s injected into the scalp to thicken hair, reduce shedding and help hair grow. “Because the PRP is derived from the patient’s blood, the procedure is 100% safe,” says Green.Red light therapy: Low-level red light therapy helps increase blood flow to your scalp, which may help prolong the growth phase of hair, says Bowles. Clinical studies show modest improvements in hair density for some people, particularly when used consistently over several months. That said, it isn’t a standalone solution for more advanced hair loss. “I consider it an adjunct therapy. It tends to be low risk when FDA-cleared devices are used as directed, but expectations should remain realistic,” says Bowles. “The best outcomes often occur when red light therapy is combined with evidence-based medical treatments such as minoxidil or hormone-targeted therapies.”

Propecia (finasteride) is an FDA-approved oral medication prescribed by a doctor that prevents testosterone from turning into dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which helps treat hair loss in men. It’s not yet FDA-approved for women, though it’s commonly prescribed off-label to women experiencing hair loss who aren’t planning to become pregnant, says Green. Dutasteride is another DHT-blocking prescription oral medication that helps increase hair density and growth, but it is not yet FDA-approved for either men or women (but still prescribed off-label to men), says Green.

Topical minoxidil, often known as its brand name Rogaine, is an FDA-approved treatment that strengthens hair follicles and promotes hair growth and retention. Dermatologists commonly prescribe oral minoxidil for both hair loss and shedding, says Green. However, you’ll need to use it consistently, or else the new hair you grew will shed again, experts say.
Ketoconazole is a common ingredient in dandruff shampoos, but paired with a hair loss treatment like minoxidil, it can potentially increase hair growth, says Ugonabo.

Spironolactone is an oral antiandrogen medication prescribed to women experiencing hair loss. It regulates androgen (male hormone) production to promote hair growth, says Green. It’s also effective for women experiencing hair loss due to Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS), she says.

Platelet-rich plasma (PRP) injections, which involves taking a sample of blood and spinning it to separate the growth factors and proteins that promote tissue regeneration and hair follicle healing, says Green. It’s injected into the scalp to thicken hair, reduce shedding and help hair grow. “Because the PRP is derived from the patient’s blood, the procedure is 100% safe,” says Green.

Red light therapy: Low-level red light therapy helps increase blood flow to your scalp, which may help prolong the growth phase of hair, says Bowles. Clinical studies show modest improvements in hair density for some people, particularly when used consistently over several months. That said, it isn’t a standalone solution for more advanced hair loss. “I consider it an adjunct therapy. It tends to be low risk when FDA-cleared devices are used as directed, but expectations should remain realistic,” says Bowles. “The best outcomes often occur when red light therapy is combined with evidence-based medical treatments such as minoxidil or hormone-targeted therapies.”

Rogaine comes recommended by all the experts I spoke to because it has topical minoxidil, which is great for both hair loss and shedding because it can increase blood flow to your hair follicles, says Ugonabo. It’s also one of the few treatments that has been FDA-approved. The active ingredient is minoxidil, which is proven to help hair growth. “Minoxidil has decades of scientific research supporting its safety and efficacy,” says Green. “[It also] increases hair follicle size, which helps new hairs grow thicker and stronger.” Though both men’s and women’s Rograine foams have 5% of minoxidil, the brand recommends men apply half a capful twice a day directly to the scalp and women apply half a capful just once a day. A lightweight topical like this one from The Ordinary can help you maintain a healthy scalp without adding buildup, so it’s great for those experiencing mild shedding, says Bowles. It has several proprietary ingredients to help hair look denser and thicker, plus it increases blood flow to the scalp, which can stimulate hair growth, according to the brand.
What are the side effects of hair loss treatments?

If you’re thinking of starting a prescription medication or over-the-counter treatment for hair loss or shedding, there are a few side effects to keep in mind, according to my experts. Each of the following treatments (with the exception of topical ketoconazole, which has minimal absorption) are not recommended during pregnancy.

Topical and oral minoxidil: Though it’s the most popular treatment for hair loss, topical minoxidil does come with a few side effects, namely scalp irritation, flaking or an initial increase in shedding as follicles shift into a growth phase, says Bowles. Oral minoxidil, which is used at low doses, can cause fluid retention, ankle swelling or changes in heart rate, she says. Because oral minoxidil was once a blood pressure medication, it’s important to have an annual physical to ensure there are no contraindications when taking it, says Green. Finasteride and dutasteride: These prescription medications block conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which may decrease libido, erectile changes or mood shifts in a very small percentage of people, says Bowles.Spironolactone: This is commonly prescribed to women with hormonal hair thinning, and side effects are rare, says Green. Some people notice menstrual irregularities, breast tenderness, dizziness or elevated potassium levels, says Bowles. Ketoconazole: Shampoos with this ingredient are generally well tolerated but may cause scalp dryness or irritation, says Bowles.

Oral minoxidil, which is used at low doses, can cause fluid retention, ankle swelling or changes in heart rate, she says. Because oral minoxidil was once a blood pressure medication, it’s important to have an annual physical to ensure there are no contraindications when taking it, says Green.

Finasteride and dutasteride: These prescription medications block conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which may decrease libido, erectile changes or mood shifts in a very small percentage of people, says Bowles.

Spironolactone: This is commonly prescribed to women with hormonal hair thinning, and side effects are rare, says Green. Some people notice menstrual irregularities, breast tenderness, dizziness or elevated potassium levels, says Bowles.
Ketoconazole: Shampoos with this ingredient are generally well tolerated but may cause scalp dryness or irritation, says Bowles.
Frequently asked questions

Though shedding may cause hair to look thinner, there are a few differences between the two. “Shedding refers to hair falling out, while thinning describes a visible decrease in hair density,” says Bowles. Hair shedding is usually due to a physical or emotional stressor, and temporary shedding can sometimes make hair appear thinner. However, the hair usually regrows once the trigger resolves. Hair thinning, on the other hand, is mostly caused by androgens (male hormones) that can shorten growth phases and create thinner hair strands, says Green.

While shedding typically happens all over the scalp, thinning hair can appear in certain areas. “Women typically experience hair thinning as widening of the part or thinning around the front of the scalp, while men typically experience hair thinning at the crown and receding hairline,” says Green.

Hormone imbalances due to stress, changes in birth control, post-pregnancy, menopause and thyroid conditions can also induce hair thinning or make it worse, she says. “In thinning hair, individual strands may become finer over time. The key difference is whether new, healthy hairs are growing back at the same rate they’re being lost,” says Bowles.

Hair loss treatments do not fundamentally change a person’s hair type, but they can influence how the hair behaves as it regrows. “Minoxidil works by prolonging the growth phase of the follicle and increasing blood flow, which can lead to new hairs that initially feel finer, softer or slightly different in texture,” says Bowles. In tightly coiled or afro-textured hair, some people notice that early regrowth appears looser or less defined, but this is usually temporary as the follicle stabilizes, she says. “Texture differences are more often related to new growth characteristics rather than permanent structural change,” says Bowles.

It’s also important to consider the formula of the treatment you’re using. “Alcohol-heavy solutions may cause dryness in curlier hair types, which can affect definition and manageability,” says Bowles. “In those cases, foam formulations or adjusting your hair care routine to support moisture balance can help.”

Research on whether scalp massages work for hair loss or hair shedding is limited. However, scalp massages can stimulate circulation and dilate blood vessels in the skin, encouraging hair growth, says Green. The increased circulation and deep cleansing can also help products like hair growth serums absorb more quickly into your scalp, she says.

Though scalp massages can be helpful, they aren’t a standalone treatment for hair loss. “A gentle massage may improve blood flow to the scalp and reduce tension, supporting overall scalp health, which can be particularly beneficial for people experiencing stress-related shedding,” says Bowles. “However, scalp massages alone are unlikely to reverse genetic or autoimmune hair loss.” It’s also important to avoid using a scalp massager aggressively because excessive traction can worsen shedding.

While minoxidil is the most proven topical treatment for hair growth, scalp oils that have rosemary, pumpkin seed, castor oil or ginseng root may help, experts say. Many lightweight oils that focus on scalp health, including those with hydrating ingredients like jojoba oil and aloe vera, can help reduce inflammation, which may help hair growth in the long run, says Bowles.
Avoid hair products with irritating ingredients like alcohol, sulfates, parabens and fragrances, which usually lead to dryness and inflammation that can ultimately worsen hair loss, says Green.

Combining treatments is common and usually beneficial. However, layering strong OTC exfoliating or medicated scalp products with prescriptions can increase the risk of irritation, says Bowles. “Oral medications also need to be reviewed alongside other prescriptions, particularly blood pressure medications or hormonal therapies,” she says.

Not all cases of hair shedding require medical treatment, especially if the cause is temporary and identifiable. Many shedding episodes resolve on their own with time, especially by reducing stress and getting proper nutrition, says Bowles. Hair loss, however, usually benefits from a medical evaluation because early treatment can help slow progression and preserve existing hair, she says.

You should consider seeing a dermatologist if your hair shedding lasts longer than six months, worsens over time or occurs without an obvious trigger. It’s also important to see a doctor if you notice patchy hair loss, scalp redness, pain or itching, says Bowles.

“Determining the underlying cause of your hair loss can be difficult, so the first step in targeting and preventing hair loss should be to consult a board-certified dermatologist who can order the necessary tests and perform a thorough exam to determine your best treatment options,” says Green.
Meet our hair care experts

At NBC Select, we work with experts who have specialized knowledge and authority based on relevant training and/or experience. We also take steps to ensure all expert advice and recommendations are made independently and without undisclosed financial conflicts of interest.

Why trust NBC Select?

I’m an updates editor at NBC Select who regularly covers beauty and personal care. I have very fine hair, and have been on the hunt for the best products to add volume, combat oil and keep frizz at bay for years. For this article, I interviewed three board-certified dermatologists about the differences between hair shedding and hair loss, plus how to treat both.

title: Lily Collins Says She’s ‘Honored and Ecstatic’ After Being Cast as Audrey Hepburn in ‘Breakfast at Tiffany’s’ Movie
summary: Lily Collins reacts to being cast as Audrey Hepburn in an upcoming film about the making of “Breakfast at Tiffany’s.”
story: Lily Collins is “honored and ecstatic” after announcing a major role she is set to take on.
The “Emily in Paris” star announced Monday, Feb. 23 that she will portray Audrey Hepburn in an upcoming movie about the making of “Breakfast at Tiffany’s.”

Beside a photo of the Academy Award winner in her iconic Holly Golightly black dress, pearls and tiara and an article about the casting, Collins revealed on Instagram that she has been working on the project for nearly a decade.

“It’s with almost 10 years of development and a lifetime of admiration and adoration for Audrey that I’m finally able share this,” Collins wrote in the caption. “Honored and ecstatic don’t begin to express how I feel…”
The actor also tagged her production company, Case Study Films, which is developing the movie alongside Imagine Entertainment, according to Deadline.

The movie will be based on Sam Wasson’s book “Fifth Avenue, 5 A.M.: Audrey Hepburn, Breakfast at Tiffany’s and the Dawn of the Modern Woman,” which details the making of the 1961 classic, the outlet reports. Alena Smith, who created the series “Dickinson,” has been tasked to write the screenplay.
Other characters expected to be included in the film are “Breakfast at Tiffany’s” novella writer Truman Capote, costume designer Edith Head, director Blake Edwards and more.
TODAY.com has reached out to Case Study Films and Imagine Entertainment for comment.

“Breakfast at Tiffany’s” told the story of Hepburn’s New York socialite Holly Golightly, who begins to fall for a man after he moves into her apartment building. However, Holly’s lifestyle threatens a potential future between her and her new neighbor.

title: John Davidson Addresses Shouting Racial Slur Due to Tourette Syndrome at BAFTAs: Read Full Statement
summary: John Davidson, a man with Tourette syndrome and the inspiration behind the film “I Swear,” has issued a statement after making several audible outbursts, including shouting the N-word, while attending the BAFTAs
story: John Davidson, a man with Tourette syndrome and the inspiration behind the film “I Swear,” has issued a statement after making several audible outbursts, including shouting the N-word, while attending the BAFTAs.

During the British awards ceremony on Sunday, Feb. 22, Davidson was in attendance to support the film he executive produced inspired by his life experience, which tells the story of a man with the neurological disorder. While sitting in the audience, one of Davidson's outbursts involved yelling the racial slur while “Sinners” stars Michael B. Jordan and Delroy Lindo were onstage presenting an award.
On Feb. 23, Davidson released a statement addressing what happened, which was published by Variety and Deadline.

“I wanted to thank BAFTA and everyone involved in the awards last night for their support and understanding and inviting me to attend the broadcast,” he said. “I appreciated the announcement to the auditorium in advance of the recording, warning everyone that my tics are involuntary and are not a reflection of my personal beliefs. I was heartened by the round of applause that followed this announcement and felt welcomed and understood in an environment that would normally be impossible for me.”

He continued, “In addition to the announcement by Alan Cumming, the BBC and BAFTA, I can only add that I am, and always have been deeply mortified if anyone considers my involuntary tics to be intentional or to carry any meaning.”
Davidson then mentioned “I Swear,” which he said, “more than any film or TV documentary, explains the origins, condition, traits and manifestations of Tourette Syndrome.”

“I have spent my life trying to support and empower the Tourette’s community and to teach empathy, kindness and understanding from others and I will continue to do so,” he added. “I chose to leave the auditorium early into the ceremony as I was aware of the distress my tics were causing.”
TODAY.com has also reached out to Davidson’s team.

Representatives for Jordan and Lindo did not immediately respond to NBC News’ requests for comment. Speaking with Vanity Fair after the event, Lindo said he and Jordan “did what we had to do” while presenting and added he wished “someone from BAFTA spoke to us afterwards."

The BBC apologized for “strong and offensive language” being heard during the awards ceremony, however the organization was criticized for leaving the slur in the two-hour-delayed broadcast. The network later said it removed the outburst from the version streaming on its web player, per NBC News.

Host Alan Cumming also told the crowd during the show, “You may have noticed some strong language in the background. This can be part of how Tourette's syndrome shows up for some people as the film explores that experience. Thanks for your understanding and helping create a respectful space for everyone.”

Cumming mentioned Tourette syndrome again later and the involuntary nature of tics. The involuntary outburst of obscene or socially inappropriate words connected to Tourette syndrome is referred to as coprolalia.
In addition to Davidson’s statement, BAFTA also apologized after the award ceremony in a statement on Feb. 23.
"At the BAFTA Film Awards last night our guests heard very offensive language that carries incomparable trauma and pain for so many," the organization said in a statement.
BAFTA confirmed that the audience was informed of Davidson’s tics prior to the show beginning.

“We take the duty of care to all our guests very seriously and start from a position of inclusion. We took measures to make those in attendance aware of the tics, announcing to the audience before the ceremony began, and throughout, that John was in the room and that they may hear strong language, involuntary noises or movements during the ceremony,” the statement continued.

BAFTA specifically issued an apology to Jordan and Lindo. “Early in the ceremony a loud tic in the form of a profoundly offensive term was heard by many people in the room. Michael B. Jordan and Delroy Lindo were on stage at the time, and we apologise unreservedly to them, and to all those impacted. We would like to thank Michael and Delroy for their incredible dignity and professionalism,” the organization said.
BAFTA also confirmed that Davidson  “chose to leave” and watch the rest of the awards ceremony from a screen.

“We would like to thank him for his dignity and consideration of others, on what should have been a night of celebration for him,” BAFTA said. “We take full responsibility for putting our guests in a very difficult situation and we apologise to all.  We will learn from this, and keep inclusion at the core of all we do, maintaining our belief in film and storytelling as a critical conduit for compassion and empathy.”

title: How Marvel Said ‘Sak Pase’ To Its First Haitian Superhero
summary: Wonder Man features a Haitian American in the lead role. Here's how they got the depiction so accurate and what it means.
story: “O! O!” I exclaimed.
A rather Haitian expression left my lips while watching Episode 3 of “Wonder Man,” Marvel’s latest Disney+ show to introduce a new superhero to the Marvel Cinematic Universe (MCU).

The show follows Simon Williams (Yahya Abdul-Mateen II), a struggling actor who is hiding ionic energy powers from an industry that would rather he be a "normal" human. Hijinks ensue, but we get to know the deeper truth behind Simon's motivations as both an actor and the first-generation child of immigrants.
In a flashback scene, Simon's mom, Martha Williams (Shola Adewusi), refers to her son as “piti mwen” in Kreyól, which means “my little one,” while he’s being checked out in a hospital room.

I was surprised by a character speaking Haitian Kreyól (pronounced “KRAY-owl”) on a mainstream Marvel show, because it’s the same language I hear my own parents speak so frequently at home as a first-generation Haitian American.
During the flashback scene, Martha asks Simon’s brother Eric (Ellis E. Fowler), “Kisa m ka fè,” meaning “What can I do?” By then, the show had my full attention.

In the original comics, Wonder Man is a white man, introduced as a foil to the Avengers. The idea that Marvel had said “goodbye” to a villain and “sak pase” (or hello) to a superhero with Haitian heritage was something I never thought I needed — until one was right in front of me.
As it turns out, it wasn't a fluke: the feeling I was having was thanks to one Marvel actor and creative consultant hired to ensure the show's cultural aspects rang true.
An Accurate Representation of Haitian-American Life
“It’s going to be just a bunch of aunties and cousins sharing war stories of growing up Haitian,” adult Simon (Yahya Abdul Mateen II) says back in Wonder Man’s present.

Later in the episode, Simon, a struggling actor, visits his mom in Pacoima, California, for her birthday along with fellow struggler Trevor Slattery (Ben Kingsley). The scene that follows is the most accurate depiction of a Haitian-American household on American television that I have ever seen.
“Tanbou’n Frape” by Haitian supergroup Lakou Mizik plays as the house’s warm pink walls call to mind a color my own aunt Janet has on her walls in Bushwick, Brooklyn.
The pleasantly mismatched lawn furniture and the judgmental-but-loving cousins all gathered to cause an almost-out-of-body experience, and I wasn’t alone.

“I have a few friends in my life who are Haitian and who reached out to me, saying like, ‘Why did you not tell me this?’” Kira Talise, a writer for “Wonder Man,” tells TODAY.com over Zoom. “But it was just really lovely to also see people who are not Haitian, or who don’t have a Caribbean ancestry, get educated and be curious.”
Talise says the show wanted to make Simon “first-generation something,” but they weren’t sure what.

“There was a lot of talk about the pressures that first-generation people in America feel, and how their parents perceive them,” she continues, adding that they wanted to focus on the pressure put on Simon to “present a certain way” or not “explore creative endeavors.”
(At this moment, I thought about the PowerPoint presentation I made for my parents when I was 17 so I could pursue the arts.)
“Our decision to make Simon Haitian came after a lot of conversation and discussion, and leveraging what resources we had to make sure that we were able to create it authentically,” Talise says.
The Man Who Brought Haiti to 'Wonder Man'

One moment really sealed the deal for me: Simon’s aunt Esther (Haitian actor Karina Bonnefil) says such a visceral line in Kreyól after a tense conversation with Slattery that you don’t really need to know the language to get the gist.
"Simon toujou ap pote fatra lakay manman," Haitian-American actor and creative consultant Jean Elie tells TODAY.com over Zoom, repeating the line with a laugh.
Translation "Simon is always bringing trash to his mother's house." Auntie!
Elie, a first-generation Haitian American who grew up in Massachusetts with two Haitian immigrant parents, was hired as a cultural consultant for the series.
He wrote lines like this for Haitian characters and their first-generation kids to make them feel authentically from the island.
“They had the scripts already written and sent me a couple of episodes,” he says.
After one authenticity read, he immediately translated parts of the dialogue into Kreyól from English.

“I marked it all up and told him, ‘Well, Haitians don’t necessarily speak about their stuff in front of people,’” Elie says, cleverly not changing a word but turning the English lines into Kreyól so Slattery wouldn't understand. (This is something my family has done countless times, especially in public.)
It was met with some pushback, but he told them that Haitian folk would take issue with the original characterization. (And we would have.)
Elie also had Martha often turn to Eric, the eldest man in the family, for guidance. “I was always translating bills and other things for my parents,” Elie recalls.

Elie, best known for playing Issa's brother Ahmal on “Insecure,” says showrunner Andrew Guest and Marvel Studios executive Brian Gay expressed their aim to make Simon’s heritage more than just a throwaway line.
Elie says he met with department heads to guide everything from wardrobe to location to the cars each character drives to help show what a lived-in Haitian experience looks like on screen.
“They took a lot of my notes, and they utilized a lot of it,” he says. “So it was really cool to see the imprint of the Haitian culture in a huge way like that.”
The show is not without critique: on social media, some criticized aspects of the show, such as its lack of authentic Haitian food and some of the accents, which Elie has responded to online.

Talise says folks have also been curious about a moment when guests enter the party, and we see them place their clothing and shoes into blue bins. This is called “barrel packing,” carried out by Caribbeans in America who fill them with clothing, food, and other supplies. Marvel’s large audience is now familiar with it, too.

“It’s an image that people could point to in reference to what Haitian culture could look like on TV, versus the stereotypical where we’re always poor or do voodoo and witchcraft,” Elie says. “One of the coolest things outside of our culture is seeing this is that other cultures are able to watch it and identify with it, which brings us closer together as people.”

Immersing the audience in worlds from Ta Lo to Jersey City is a Marvel specialty, and this time, my actual world was represented on the screen; It made me feel that my own life had a little more wonder.
Sak pase, Wonder Man.

title: Why Katherine Heigl 'Debated' Posting a Tribute to Eric Dane 'For Days'
summary: Katherine Heigl wrote a post mourning the loss of “Grey’s Anatomy” co-star Eric Dane.
story: Katherine Heigl is sharing a reflection on grief as she mourns the loss of her former “Grey’s Anatomy” co-star Eric Dane.
Dane passed away at the age of 53 on Feb. 19 following a battle with ALS.
While playing the role of Dr. Izzie Stevens on “Grey’s Anatomy,” Heigl got to know Dane, who played Dr. Mark Sloan, aka McSteamy.
In a post shared to her Facebook page, Heigl said she was hesitant to speak out publicly about his death at first.

“I debated this post for days. I have never been comfortable grieving publicly. I share my tears, my stories and my memories with those I’m closest to and more times than not I share them only with the heavens alone. But that silence can often look like a lack of care which I could not abide here,” she wrote.
Heigl addressed Dane's wife, Rebecca Gayheart, and their two daughters directly in the post, acknowledging that there’s only so much one can say in the wake of loss.

“All the ‘I’m sorries’ and ‘my prayers are with you’ ring a bit hollow in the midst of so much pain and grief. So I will say the only thing that has ever brought me peace when I’ve lost someone I love,” she wrote.

Heigl quoted the Robert Frost poem “Nothing Gold Can Stay,” and said that she has the poem tattooed on her body in her parents’ handwriting so she will “never forget that the moment of gold was worth it.”

“‘Nature’s first green is gold, her hardest hue to hold. Her early leaf’s a flower; but only so an hour. Then leaf subsides to leaf. So Eden sank to grief, so Dawn goes down to day. Nothing gold can stay,’” she wrote.
Heigl then brought the poem’s message back to her experience working with Dane on the set of the ABC medical drama.
“I will never forget Eric’s gold and I will forever be grateful I got to bask in it for even a brief moment in time. Thank you for sharing him with us,” she wrote.
The 47-year-old ended her tribute by speaking directly to her late co-star.
“God Speed my old friend…here’s to your next great adventure,” she wrote.

Heigl joins several of Dane’s co-stars who have paid tribute to the late actor after his death. Patrick Dempsey, who played Dr. Derek “McDreamy” Shepherd on the show, spoke to Dane’s passing while appearing on Britain’s “The Chris Evans Breakfast Show.”
“Yeah, it’s hard to put into words. I feel really so sad for his children,” he said.
Dempsey spoke about Dane’s “great sense of humor” as an actor and co-star and said they “got along instantly.”
The actor also applauded the work Dane did advocating for awareness of ALS.

“The real loss is for us who don’t have him any more,” he said. “He did an incredible job at bringing awareness to this horrible disease and those remaining days, which was really brave of him to do.”

title: Tracy Morgan Reveals the Gift He Gave 35 'Reggie Dinkins' Cast and Crew Members
summary: Tracy Morgan said he bought the crew of The Fall and Rise of Reggie Dinkins necklaces.
story: Tracy Morgan considers the crew on his new NBC show "The Fall and Rise of Reggie Dinkins" to be like family — and he gave them some bling to make it official.

The comedian shared on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle on Feb. 23 that he gave out special gifts to everyone working on the sitcom. Sheinelle Jones asked him about his blingy necklace, which shows the torso of a football player in a No. 24 jersey. The necklace also appears to have the name "Dinkins."
Morgan then he revealed that he had similar ones made for the crew.

"I bought about 35 of these, real diamonds, 18 karat gold, for everyone involved in the show," he said. "This was my way of saying thank you. Sometimes I don’t have the words to express it, so I showed them thank you. I really appreciate it."

The number 24 is the jersey number worn by Morgan's character, Reggie Dinkins, on the show. Morgan plays a disgraced former football star trying to change his public perception by having a documentary crew make a film about him. The director is played by "Harry Potter" and "Weird: The Al Yankovic Story" star Daniel Radcliffe.

Morgan, 57, said he bought necklaces for "the cameramen, craft services, everybody." He tried to set the tone of a family atmosphere by having the crew at his home for dinner a day before shooting began.
"Everyone was comfortable, got to know each other, speaking and talking, and I’m just looking around (thinking), we ain't a team," he said. "We’re a family."

"I told all the cameramen and everybody on the set, I will get to know your name, please be patient with me," he continued. "I want to talk to people like the cameraman, 'Yo, what up Craig, or what up Derek?' Stuff like that."
Morgan called the necklaces "a show of appreciation." He added that he also brings a boom box to blast music on the set.
"It’s all about energy," he said. "Whether it’s good energy or bad energy, bring it to the set, we could use it. We feed off everyone’s energy."
Much of the energy on the show plays off the unlikely pairing of Morgan and Radcliffe.
"A guy from the UK and a street guy from Brooklyn get together, how does that happen?" Morgan said.
Morgan told Craig Melvin earlier on TODAY on Feb. 23 that he had never met Radcliffe before doing the show with him.
"People need to see me and Daniel Radcliffe coexisting in that space right now in the world," he said. "And we're making beautiful music together."

"I didn't know him, but I have children, and we went to see all the 'Harry Potter' movies. And carpe diem. You have to seize the moment to be a cool dad, and now I'm a cool dad, I'm working with Harry Potter."
The show was created by Morgan's former "30 Rock" collaborators, Robert Carlock and Sam Means, with Tina Fey as one of the executive producers.
"That's my sister," Morgan said. "She knows what she means to me."
Morgan, who survived a near-fatal car accident in 2014, is grateful to still be able to do what he loves and make people laugh with the new show.

"To be hit by a truck doing 75 miles per hour, and what I went through, I’m just glad to be here," he told Craig. "I don’t want a job. I want pursuits. I tell my children that all the time, you want pursuits. You want to find out what your heart loves, and you want to pursue it. And comedy is my heart, and I love to make you guys laugh."

title: When Does 'Paradise' Season 2 Come Out? How to Watch
summary: “Paradise” returns for Season 2. Here is when it will premiere, what time and what people can expect.
story: “Paradise” is back and the question on everyone’s mind is: Is anyone alive above ground and did Xavier make it out of the bunker?
Season 2 of the Emmy-nominated Hulu series returns with three new episodes, picking up after Sterling K Brown’s Agent Xavier Collins planned his escape to find his wife.
After Season 1's catastrophic events, unexpected deaths and twists and turns, viewers can't wait to see what the new “Paradise” episodes have in store.
Brown previously told TODAY.com that the new season would explore what is life like above ground.
“That is something that ... we will be exploring,” he said in February 2025. “I can tell you that much, but I think the main exploration and the main question will be, is his wife really alive?”
Find out when and what time Season 2 of “Paradise” premieres, as well as what is set to happen and new cast members.
When Does 'Paradise' Season 2 Premiere?
Season 2 of “Paradise” premieres on Monday, Feb. 23 with three new episodes dropping on Hulu and Disney+ at 3 a.m. ET/12 a.m. PT.
What Is the Episode Schedule for ‘Paradise’ Season 2?
Season 2 consists of eight episodes, with new episodes dropping weekly until the March 30 finale.
Below, find a schedule of the episode releases.
Feb. 23: Season 2, Episode 1 ("Graceland")Feb. 23: Season 2, Episode 2 (“Mayday”)
Feb. 23: Season 2, Episode 1 ("Graceland")
Feb. 23: Season 2, Episode 2 (“Mayday”)

Feb. 23: Season 2, Episode 3 ("Another Day in Paradise")March 2: Season 2, Episode 4 (“A Holy Charge”)March 9: Season 2, Episode 5March 16: Season 2, Episode 6March 23: Season 2, Episode 7March 30: Season 2, Episode 8
Feb. 23: Season 2, Episode 3 ("Another Day in Paradise")
March 2: Season 2, Episode 4 (“A Holy Charge”)
March 9: Season 2, Episode 5
March 16: Season 2, Episode 6
March 23: Season 2, Episode 7
March 30: Season 2, Episode 8
What Will Happen in Season 2 of 'Paradise'?
Season 2 of “Paradise” will see Xavier continue the search for his wife, Teri, out in the world after escaping the underground bunker in Colorado.
In the new episodes, he will learn “how people survived the three years since The Day,” according to the synopsis.
Meanwhile, back in Paradise, “the social fabric frays as the bunker deals with the aftermath of Season 1, and new secrets are uncovered about the city’s origins.”
Who Are the New Cast Members In Season 2 of 'Paradise'?
Brown returns along with Julianne Nicholson, Sarah Shahi, Nicole Brydon Bloom, Krys Marshall, Enuka Okuma and James Marsden, among others.
New cast members include Shailene Woodley as Annie, a tour guide in Memphis, Tennessee, who survives the massive catastrophe.
Thomas Doherty joins as Link, described as a “tough and charming” leader of a group of survivors, per Disney+. Raymond Cham Jr. and Ryan Michelle Bathé will also appear in Season 2.

title: Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy's Biographer Shares Her Thoughts on 'Love Story'
summary: Carolyn Bessette Kennedy's biographer Elizabeth Beller, whose book Once Upon a Time inspired Love Story, shares her take on the show.
story: “Love Story,” the new limited series about Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy and John F. Kennedy Jr.’s relationship, has sparked debate and conversation about casting since its February premiere. (Actually, make that months before its premiere, when fans criticized photos of the actors on set, questioning whether they measured up to the real people.)
Elizabeth Beller, author of the 2024 biography “Once Upon A Time: The Captivating Life of Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy,” weighed in on the series and its portrayal of Bessette and Kennedy.

“I know from what I’ve seen so far that they’ve handled the show with a lot of sensitivity,” Beller tells TODAY.com. “I think they took the fact that it was dramatizing real people with their responsibility. And they took it very seriously.”
The series revisits one of America’s most mythologized couples, from their courtship through to the tragic 1999 plane crash where they, along with Bessette's sister Lauren Bessette, died.
“From what I’ve seen, it feels like they were respectful, and that was great to see,” she said. “I think they did a really great job with thoughtful, respectful storytelling.”
At the same time, Beller has a reminder for viewers: “Love Story” is not a documentary. Dramatizations inevitably condense timelines, heighten emotion and interpret private moments.
“I think a thing to keep in mind is that it’s not a documentary, but a dramatization, which they do say at one point,” she says.
Creator Connor Hines told the Associated Press the show's research went beyond Beller's book.
“We obviously had Elizabeth’s book, but I read every single newspaper article that was written about them, every tell-all,” said Hines.

“Obviously, all of that material has to be taken with a grain of salt, and you have to be very judicious in terms of how you extract some of this information, because people have their own memories, people have their own slights, but we were exhaustive and diligent in how we approached this and I think that will ring true when people watch it," he continued.
Hines also told Variety that he has not been in touch with the Kennedy family, and instead relied on research.

“As a writer, it’s more healthy and effective to have some distance from the subject matter,” he told Variety at the show's premiere. “It allows you to be a lot more objective in how you approach the material, versus when you’re talking to people personally, you feel a sense of responsibility of relaying exactly what they’re telling you because they’ve given you their time.”

As renewed interest in Bessette’s life continues, from TikTok style tributes to fresh debates about her marriage, “Love Story” adds another chapter to the evolving cultural conversation. And according to the biographer who studied her life most closely, it’s one told with respect.

title: Baby Pygmy Hippo Joins TODAY Live From Arizona Zoo For Her Name Reveal
summary: Arizona’s Wildlife World Zoo revealed the name of its new baby pygmy hippo, exclusively on TODAY.
story: Arizona’s Wildlife World Zoo has revealed the name of its new baby pygmy hippo after receiving thousands of votes from visitors and fans.
The animal, born Jan. 22, has been named Jellybean after the name collected 81.3% of the vote. Cadbury (7.3%) placed second, Twixi (6.9%) took third and Taffy came in fourth (4.6%).
Arizona’s Wildlife World Zoo, Aquarium & Safari Park, President Kristy Hayden appeared on TODAY Feb. 23 next to Jellybean's watering hole, and the baby hippo sat perched in Hayden's lap.

"OK, so the moment we've all been waiting for, without any further ado — world, I'd like you to meet little Jellybean," Hayden said to a round of cheers from TODAY's Jenna Bush Hager, Carson Daly, Hoda Kotb, Craig Melvin and Al Roker.
"I don't know if we could have a cuter name for this adorable little hippo," Hayden continued.
Pygmy hippos are an endangered species, with an estimated 2,500 still in the wild. Births are a rare occurrence, and Jellybean’s arrival can help play a part in their conservation.
Jellybean, along with mom Lollipop, will be on exhibit during regular hours at Arizona’s Wildlife World Zoo, which is located in the town of Litchfield Park, about 20 miles outside of Phoenix.
Jellybean was 11 pounds at birth, but was up to 33 pounds at last week's most recent check-in. Pygmy hippos can grow to anywhere between 350 and 600 pounds.
Jellybean’s birth comes after the Khao Kheow Open Zoo in Thailand welcomed its own baby pygmy hippo in July 2024 named Moo Deng that went viral and sparked an influx of visitors to that facility.
“Moo Deng may not know (of her popularity), but her mom might be surprised as before it was quiet,” the keeper told TODAY in September 2024.

title: The BAFTAS Are Back! How to Watch the Awards Show in the U.S.
summary: What time are the BAFTA awards and how can you watch them in the U.S.? Here's a guide to watching the award show for American viewers.
story: This weekend, movie stars will head across the pond to attend the 2026 BAFTAs.

The ceremony, which will mark the 79th British Academy Film Awards, celebrates the best national and foreign language films of the past year. Actor and “The Traitors” host Alan Cumming is set to emcee the event, held at the Royal Festival Hall in London.

Actors David Jonsson and Aimee Lou Wood first announced the nominees for the 2026 BAFTAs on Jan. 27. Jonsson, who starred in “The Long Walk” last year, won the EE Rising Star Award at the previous ceremony and Wood had a breakout year thanks to her role in Season 3 of “The White Lotus.”
For the 2026 ceremony, “One Battle After Another” leads the field with 14 nominations. “Sinners” and “Hamnet” closely follow with 13 and 11 each, respectively.

Timothée Chalamet, Michael B. Jordan, Robert Aramayo, Leonardo DiCaprio, Ethan Hawke and Jesse Plemons all scored nominations in the leading actor category. For the leading actress category, Jessie Buckley, Rose Byrne, Kate Hudson, Chase Infiniti, Renate Reinsve and Emma Stone are going head to head.
Want to see who walks away with the prestigious award? Find out how to tune in, below.
When Are the 2026 BAFTAs?
The 2026 BAFTAs will be held on Sunday, Feb. 22 in London at Southbank Centre’s Royal Festival Hall.
What Time Are the 2026 BAFTAS?
The awards show airs in the U.S. at 8 p.m. ET / 5 p.m. PT.
How to Watch and Stream the 2026 BAFTAs
Movie fans tuning in from the U.S. can watch the show on E!
Those in the UK will be able to watch the ceremony live at 7 p.m. GMT on BBC One and BBC iPlayer.
Who Are the 2026 BAFTA Nominees?

Many actors and movies that have been repeatedly mentioned this award season have also been recognized by the BAFTAs. The best film category at the 2026 BAFTAs includes “Hamet,” “Marty Supreme,” “One Battle After Another,” “Sentimental Value” and “Sinners.”
Check out a few of the other categories below, and see the full list of nominations here.

title: Who Is Rebecca Gayheart? What to Know About Eric Dane's Wife
summary: Eric Dane has been married to Rebecca Gayheart since 2004, although she filed for divorce in 2018. Here's what to know about Dane's wife and their marriage.
story: Before his death at the age of 53, Eric Dane spent his final days with his wife Rebecca Gayheart and their two daughters, Billie and Georgia.
The actor announced that he had been diagnosed with ALS, a progressive nervous system disease that “affects nerve cells in the brain and spinal cord,” per the Mayo Clinic, last year.

Dane and his wife’s relationship shifted from romance to friendship in the final years of his life. Gayheart filed for divorce from Dane in 2018, but dismissed her petition in March 2025, just a month before Dane announced on April 10 that he had been diagnosed with ALS.
“We are best friends. We are really close. We are great co-parents,” Gayheart told E! News the day before Dane shared news of his diagnosis.
Although Dane and Gayheart were no longer a romantic couple at the time of his death, they remained close.
“We really figured out the formula to staying a family and I think our kids are benefiting greatly from it and we are as well,” Gayheart said.
In a Netflix special titled “Famous Last Words: Eric Dane,” Dane spoke about the status of his relationship with Gayheart.
“Their mother and I are no longer together. We’re still best friends but we’re not together,” he said.
Read on to learn more about Gayheart.
Gayheart and Dane Wed in 2004 and and Split in 2018

Gayheart and Dane met in 2003. Like Dane, Gayheart is an actor. She got her big break on the daytime drama “Loving” in 1992 and later played Luke Perry’s girlfriend (and eventual bride) on “Beverly Hills, 90210.” She also appeared on the TV shows “Nip/Tuck” and “Vanished,” and movies like “Once Upon a Time in Hollywood,” “Scream 2,” “Jawbreaker” and “Harvard Man.”
In his “Famous Last Words” Netflix special, Dane described how he was enchanted by his future wife when he first met her.
“I met Rebecca and I turned to her friend and I said, ‘I’m going to marry that girl,’” he recalled.
Gayheart was engaged to another man at that point, but she later started dating Dane. The couple tied the knot in October 2004.
Gayheart filed for divorce in February 2018 after nearly 14 years of marriage, citing irreconcilable differences, E! News reported at the time.

“We will continue our friendship and work as a team to co-parent our two beautiful girls as they are the most important thing in the world to us,” the pair said in a statement at the time. “We kindly ask that you respect our privacy during this time as we navigate the next phase of our lives.”
Gayheart dismissed her petition for divorce in March 2025, a month before Dane announced his ALS diagnosis.
Gayheart and Dane Have Spoken Openly About Their Separation
In December 2025, Gayheart penned an essay for The Cut and offered insight into how her family was doing amid Dane’s diagnosis with ALS. She also offered insight into their decision to separate.

“We had a really lovely marriage for a long time — we were married for 15 years — we created two beautiful girls. But also, lots of s--- went crazy in our relationship, and it wasn’t good. We separated, but we never got a divorce; we were about to and then we didn’t,” she said.
Gayheart revealed that the couple hadn’t lived in the same house for eight years and noted that they both had dated other people in that time.

“It’s a very complicated relationship, one that’s confusing for people. Our love may not be romantic, but it’s a familial love. Eric knows that I am always going to want the best for him. That I’m going to do my best to do right by him. And I know he would do the same for me,” she wrote.
In his “Famous Last Words” Netflix special, Dane said he and Gayheart still loved each other “deeply.”

“I just think we don’t want to live with each other. But there’s a lot of love there. I will never, by the time anybody sees this, have fallen in love with another woman as deeply as I fell in love with Rebecca. She is the mother of my children,” he said.
When asked why the marriage didn’t work out, Dane offered the following response.
“I think Rebecca was more willing to show up and do her part than I was,” he said.

Dane then elaborated on his statement, saying, “I don’t have that gene that just makes you want to keep going regardless of what’s happening. I’m like, ‘If there’s a hole in the boat, don’t try to patch the hole. Scuttle the damn thing and go find a new one.”
They Share Two Daughters
Gayheart and Dane are parents to two daughters: Billie, 15, and Georgia, 14.
Despite going their separate ways in 2018, Gayheart and Dane co-parented their kids together.

“My ex Eric and I, we are friends and we are doing our best to co-parent and maintain a family, even though we’re not married. Legally we’re still married, but separated,” Gayheart told People in July 2019. “And I think we’re doing a pretty good job of it. Life is just good. There are so many exciting things happening.”
Gayheart, Dane and their daughters vacationed together in August 2022.
“This is us, family vacay 2022,” Gayheart captioned an Instagram photo of her family in Paris.
The exes lived 12 minutes away from each other and spent a lot of time together as a family with their two daughters after their separation.
“He made it very clear that he wants to spend time with his family as much as possible, and I am committed to facilitating that,” Gayheart told The Cut.
During his “Famous Last Words” special on Netflix, Dane said he remained a constant presence in his daughters’ lives after he and his wife separated.
He said he worried whether he was spending enough time with his girls.
“Just by virtue of the distance and us living in separate homes there was a lot of time lost there. But I made sure that I could be there as much as possible and certainly when it counts,” he said.
Gayheart Supported Dane During His ALS Battle
In her essay for The Cut, Gayheart detailed the toll Dane’s illness had taken on him and his family and outlined the role she played in supporting him throughout the challenging time.

“Eric has 24/7 nurses now. Just figuring out the health-care system is its own thing — the health-insurance company will deny you what you’re asking for and you have to appeal and then you have to apply again. With the nurses, the woman from his insurance said to me, ‘You can keep applying, and I’ll keep denying,’ I was like, Oh? F that. I made it my mission. I was ‘locked in,’ as the kids would say. I crashed out, and then I locked it in. And we got it approved after two appeals,” she wrote.
Gayheart Has Called Her Marriage to Dane a ‘Huge Success’
Gayheart has repeatedly expressed pride in the close friendship she and Dane maintained after their their romantic relationship ended.
Gayheart also said that both she and Dane considered their marriage to have been a “success.”

“It’s just a season. It wasn’t a failure. It was a huge success,” Gayheart told E! News. “We were married for, I mean, we are still married, but together for 15 years, and we had two beautiful kids so I think that’s a successful relationship, and that’s how we look at it.”

title: Patrick Dempsey Pays Tribute to ‘McSteamy,’ His Longtime ‘Grey's Anatomy’ Co-Star Eric Dane
summary: Patrick Dempsey remembered Eric Dane, his former “Grey’s Anatomy” co-star who died at the age of 53 announcing his ALS diagnosis last year.
story: Patrick Dempsey is mourning the loss of his former “Grey’s Anatomy” co-star Eric Dane, who died at the age of 53 on Feb. 19 after announcing his ALS diagnosis in 2025.
“I just woke up this morning and I was very sad to read the news,” Dempsey said Feb. 20 during an appearance on Britain’s “The Chris Evans Breakfast Show.”
Dempsey noted his grief and what Dane's death means for  his family.
“Yeah, it’s hard to put into words. I feel really so sad for his children,” he said, referring to his daughters, Billie and Georgia, whom he shared with his ex Rebecca Gayheart.
Dempsey said he and Dane had recently been in contact, but he knew his condition was worsening.

“I was corresponding with him, we were texting, so I spoke to him about a week ago and some friends of ours went in to see him and he was really starting to lose his ability to speak,” he said. “He was bedridden and it was very hard for him to swallow, so the quality of his life was deteriorating so rapidly.”

On “Grey’s Anatomy,” Dane played Dr. Mark Sloan, nicknamed “McSteamy,” while Dempsey portrayed Dr. Derek Shepherd, known for his part as “McDreamy.” Dempsey debuted on the show in the pilot in 2005, but his character died in Season 11 in 2015, although the character has appeared sporadically since.
Dane first appeared on the ABC drama in Season 2 before leaving the show in Season 8 in 2012, with his character also dying.
In the interview Friday, Dempsey recalled Dane's "great sense of humor" as an actor and co-star.

"He was easy to work with. We got along instantly. You know, first scene was him, you know, in all his glory, coming out of the bathroom with the towel on, looking amazing, making me feel completely out of shape and insignificant," Dempsey said.
But he said there "was never really any competition."

“There was just this wonderful mutual respect," Dempsey said. "He’s wickedly intelligent and I’m always going to remember those moments of fun that we had together and celebrate the joy that he did bring to people’s lives."
Dempsey also praised Dane's advocacy work.

“The real loss is for us who don’t have him anymore," he said. "He did an incredible job at bringing awareness to this horrible disease and those remaining days, which was really brave of him to do. And it just reminds us that we all have to celebrate every day like it’s our last day, because I am at the age now where I am wearing glasses because I can’t see.

“... It’s something we have to remember, certainly in a world with there is just so much crisis and there’s so much tragedy that we really need to be grateful for every moment that we have. Spend time with our families, do things that are better, that are the betterment of other people, to be of service, to be kind, to be loving."

title: Eric Dane Shares a Message for His 2 Daughters in His Last Interview
summary: In his last interview special shared on Netflix, Eric Dane said the four life lessons he learned from ALS, directed at daughters Billie and Georgia.
story: Before his death at the age of 53, Eric Dane shared four life lessons with his daughters, Billie and Georgia, in his final interview.

The actor sat down with Brad Falchuk for a Netflix special titled "Famous Last Words: Eric Dane" and reflected on his life and career during an hour-long conversation. Throughout the interview, Dane discussed the impact his ALS diagnosis had on his life and his worldview.
At the end of the special, Falchuk left the room to allow Dane the opportunity to speak to the camera alone and share a message for his daughters.
"I want to tell you four things that I've learned from this disease and I hope you won't just listen to me. I hope you'll hear me," Dane said.
Dane went on to share the four life lessons he learned from his ALS diagnosis, which he wanted to impart on his daughters.
#1 : 'Live in the Present'
Dane began by encouraging his girls to live "right now in the present."

"It's hard but I learned to do that. For years, I'd wander off mentally, lost in my head for a long time, wallowing and worrying in self pity, shame and doubt and replay decisions, second guess myself. 'I should've done this. I never should've done that," he explained.
The actor then outlined why this life lesson is particularly important.
"The past contains regrets. The future remains unknown. So you have to live now. The present is all you have. Treasure it, cherish every moment," he said.
#2: 'Fall in Love'
Dane also expressed a desire for his daughters to fall in love, in multiple ways.

"Fall in love. Not necessarily with a person, although I do recommend that as well. But fall in love with something. Find your passion, your joy, find the thing that makes you want to get up in the morning," he said.
The father of two then explained that he fell in love "for the first time" when he was around his daughters' ages.
"I fell in love with acting. That love eventually got me through my darkest hours, my darkest days, my darkest years. I still love my work. I still look forward to it," he said.
Dane said his work didn't "define" him, but it still excited him.
"Find something. Find something that excites you. Find your path, your purpose, your dream, then go for it. Really go for it," he said.
#3: 'Find Your People'
While outlining his wishes for his daughters, Dane said that he wanted them to choose their friends wisely.

"Find your people and allow them to find you, and then give yourselves to them. The best of them will give back to you, no judgment, no conditions, no questions asked. I'm so thankful for my very close family and friends," he said.
Dane went on to describe how his friends "stepped up" during his diagnosis.

"I can't do the little things I used to do. I can't drive around town and go to the gym and get coffee. Now I've learned to embrace alternatives. My friends come to me. We eat together, watch a game, listen to music. They don't do anything special. They just show up," he said.
Dane encouraged his daughters to love their friends and "hang onto them."
"They will entertain you, guide you, help you, support you. And some will save you," he said.
#4: 'Fight With Every Ounce of Your Being '
Dane asked his daughters to channel his resilience when they face their own life challenges.
"Fight with every ounce of your being and with dignity. When you face challenges, health or otherwise, fight. Never give up. Fight until your last breath," he said.
Dane said that ALS was "slowly taking" his body but said the disease would "never take my spirit."
"The two of you are different people, but you're both strong and resilient. You inherited resiliency from me. That's my superpower," he said.
“You can face hell with dignity. Fight, girls, and hold your heads high.
Eric Dane's Message to his daughters
Dane added some levity to the heavy moment and joked that he was on his 15th life at this point, because he always persevered.
"When something unexpected hits you — and it will, because that's life — fight and face it with honesty, integrity and grace, even if it feels or seems surmountable," he said.
"I hope I've demonstrated that you can face anything. You can face the end of your days. You can face hell with dignity. Fight, girls, and hold your heads high."

title: Kelly Clarkson Opens Up About End of Her Talk Show, What’s Next with ‘The Voice’
summary: Kelly Clarkson has opened up about why she ended her daytime talk show as she gets ready to be a coach on the upcoming Season 29 of “The Voice.”
story: Kelly Clarkson has opened up about why she ended her daytime talk show as she gets ready to be a coach on the upcoming Season 29 of “The Voice.”
The Grammy-winning singer spoke alongside fellow “Voice” coach John Legend on TODAY Feb. 20 about why she decided now was a good time to bring the show to a close after seven seasons.

“I think everybody probably gets the timing,” she told Carson Daly. “Our family life, the dynamic changed a bit, and it has changed for a minute now. You got kids, we’ve all got kids, and it’s one of those things when you kind of start seeing life as how precious it is, too. It’s like, ‘You know what, I’m really busy.’”

Clarkson is a mom to daughter River Rose, 11, and son Remy, 9, whom she welcomed with her late ex-husband, Brandon Blackstock. He died at 48 in August 2025 after three years of being treated for cancer.

“I was like, ‘I know everybody thinks she’s like quitting,’” Clarkson said about the show. “I’m like, ‘I still have other jobs, so I’m still doing stuff.’ There’s just too much on the plate, so I was like you know what, it’s time to pull back.”
The decision was a difficult one for Clarkson between the success of the show and the close-knit relationship with the staff.

“That’s what was really hard,” she said. “I work with a lot of people here, too. The crew’s been incredible, it wasn’t that the show isn’t doing well. That’s what kind of sucked, everything was going well, and that’s what was really hard.
“That was a really hard thing for me, but an easy decision as a momma.”

Clarkson and Legend also gave a preview of Season 29 of “The Voice” — which features a new wrinkle, in that there will only be three coaches instead of the usual four. Out of her nine previous seasons on the show, Clarkson is a four-time winning coach.
“I have won the most, if you’re counting,” she joked. “It was part of my pitch.”

Her team will face off against teams coached by two other icons of the hit NBC talent show, Legend and Adam Levine, in “The Voice: Battle of Champions.” Clarkson also said on TODAY the show will be bringing back some past champions this season as she makes her return to “The Voice” for the first time since Season 23 in 2023.
"I've been gone for a while, so it worked, scheduling," she said.

As a veteran of “The Voice,” Clarkson still enjoys the Blind Audition segments where the coaches sit in swivel chairs with their backs turned and can only hear the singers performing behind them but can’t look at them.

“I love games in general, and I love just being competitive in general, but I do love not knowing who’s behind you,” she said. “And then you have this idea in your head, and you turn around, and I am 90% of the time wrong, like every time. You keep thinking, we’ve done this so many times, there can’t possibly be someone just incredible, and wow.”
“Every season, America just keeps giving us great singers,” Legend said.
The new season premieres on Feb. 23 at 9 p.m. ET on NBC and will be available to stream on Peacock the next day.
Clarkson’s return to “The Voice” comes less than a month after she announced on Instagram Feb. 2 that “The Kelly Clarkson Show” was coming to an end after seven seasons.

“I have been extremely fortunate to work with such an outstanding group of people at (‘The Kelly Clarkson Show’), both in Los Angeles and New York,” she wrote. “There have been so many amazing moments and shows over these seven seasons. I am forever grateful and honored to have worked alongside the greatest band and crew you could hope for, all the talent and inspiring people who have shared their time and lives with us, all the fans who have supported our show, and to NBC for always being such a supportive and incredible partner.”
She shared that her family was a key part of the decision.
“Stepping away from the daily schedule will allow me to prioritize my kids, which feels necessary and right for this next chapter of our lives,” she wrote.

New episodes of Season 7 of “The Kelly Clarkson Show” will air through fall 2026, according to a press release. Since debuting in 2019, the show has racked up two dozen Daytime Emmy awards and achieved continued virality for Clarkson’s signature “Kellyoke” performances.

title: ‘The Traitors’ Season 4, Episode 10 Recap: Who Was Banished and Who Was Murdered?
summary: Did two Traitors successfully make it to the finale? Or, will the Faithfuls have the upper hand going into the last episode? Read a recap of Episode 10 to find out.
story: Warning: Spoilers from Season 4, Episode 10 of “The Traitors.”
The penultimate episode in Season 4 of “The Traitors” saw original Traitor Rob Rausch struggling for the first time in the game as he tried to manage his paranoia and avoid suspicion.

At the start of episode 10, the “Love Island USA” star and singer Eric Nam, a recent recruit to the turret, plotted who to murder next. The two attempted to map out a plan that would ensure they both make it to the finale, airing Feb. 26, along with the reunion. But with only eight players remaining, it was becoming more and more challenging to throw the Faithfuls off their scent. So, after weeks of keeping a target off his back, Rausch was finally questioned by the Faithfuls.

The Faithfuls were desperate to unmask another Traitor because a crucial Round Table was on the way. As host Alan Cumming explained, it was the last time a banished player would reveal their identity before leaving. So, did two Traitors successfully make it to the finale? Or, will the Faithfuls have the upper hand going into the last episode?
Read a recap of Episode 10 to find out what happened.
The Walls Are Closing in on Rob?

Instead of beginning in the turret, Episode 10 opens with the players who weren’t up for murder arriving for breakfast. “Love Island” star Maura Higgins and Rausch are the first two to enter the breakfast room. In his confessional, Rausch reveals that he has been feeling the weight of all his lying and scheming from the past few weeks. He adds that he feels bad for deceiving Higgins and the other Faithfuls who blindly trust him.

Next, fellow Traitor Nam and the remaining Faithfuls — “Dancing With the Stars” pro Mark Ballas, Olympic figure skaters Tara Lipinski and Johnny Weir and “Survivor” winner Natalie Anderson — all arrive, meaning “Top Chef” host Kristen Kish was murdered.
In a flashback, viewers see Kish learning her fate after turning a creepy jack-in-the-box toy in the dungeon that confirmed she was murdered.

“My time here has come to an end, but I’m at peace,” she says in her final confessional. “I was the most faithful of the Faithfuls. I don’t think many people doubted that and I think the Traitors knew that.”

Then, viewers learn that Rausch and Nam decided to murder Kish even though she threw out the singer’s name last episode because they were convinced they could get the players to focus on Anderson instead.

Cumming appears and announces that tomorrow will be the final day in the castle. Before the mission begins, Lipinski tells Anderson that she is starting to doubt that Rausch is a Faithful. She mentions that “The Real Housewives of Potomac” star Candiace Dillard Bassett voted for Rausch twice before she was outed as a Traitor. Anderson agrees with Lipinski and adds that she thinks she hasn’t been murdered because Rausch has been protecting her in the turret. Meanwhile, Rausch and Nam are carrying out their plan of making Anderson look guilty by mentioning her name to Ballas.
On the way to the mission, Higgins alerts Rausch that Lipinski and Anderson are suspicious of him.
Rob Spins Out at the Mission

Going into the mission, Rausch says he wants to secure the shield to ensure he and Nam can murder anyone they want. At the mission, the players have to strap themselves into a spinning ride and hold on. When the ride stops, they must find bags of gold hidden inside stuffed animals scattered around the fair grounds. Then, they must return to the ride for another spin.

But, Cumming has a twist up his sleeve. He doesn’t tell the players that each round one chair on the ride will be removed, eliminating the last player to return. The first round begins and Rausch finds his bag of gold last. When he learns he has been eliminated, Rausch becomes upset, directs his anger toward Ballas and complains about needing a shield, making some of the players question his behavior.
The rounds continue and the last player remaining is Nam, who earns the shield. The group adds $24,500 to the prize pot.
An Emotional Round Table

While returning from the mission, Anderson continues to suggest that Rausch is a Traitor and mentions that his reactions during the mission felt performative. Lipinski also says Rausch’s demeanor has changed, but she worries about being too vocal at the Round Table since she has been wrong every week.

At the Round Table, Cumming tells the player that this is the last time a banished player will reveal if they are a Traitor or a Faithful. The Round Table begins and Lipinski says she doesn’t want to lead the group in the wrong direction, so she will take a back seat. Rausch calls out Anderson for being distant recently. She counters by pointing out he hasn’t been confiding in her. She also brings up her evidence: Candiace’s two votes against him.

But, Ballas and Higgins say that Anderson could be a Traitor that decided to murder all the gamers to make herself appear vulnerable. When Weir and Lipinski remain mostly quiet, Anderson feels manipulated and says she regrets sharing her Rausch theory.
Prior to the vote, Rausch uses his Dagger, allowing him to vote twice. He votes for the “Survivor” alum and all the other players also vote to banish Anderson, who votes for Lipinski.

While standing on the Circle of Truth, Anderson says, “You know, my whole life I’ve wanted to be so strong and I try really hard to present as tough. And sometimes it comes with a negative of being judged, of being ruthless, cutthroat. But today, a gamer got played, I’m a Faithful. So, good job guys.”
A Scorching Murder

After Anderson leaves, Lipinski begins to cry. “I truthfully don’t want to play this game anymore. I feel like a fool. I have made mistakes. But seriously, I am OK with being murdered tonight,” she tells the group before Ballas comforts her.

Rausch says in his confessional that that was the toughest Round Table so far but he “had to do what he had to do.” He then joins Ballas, who is now questioning why Nam didn’t speak at all during the Round Table. Weir voices the same concern in his confessional and theorizes that Rausch and Nam are the two remaining Traitors.
In the turret, Cumming interrupts Nam and Rausch’s discussion about who to murder. He tells them their last kill will be “a scorcher.”

Cumming says that after the Traitors make their decision, all the players will meet outside. “There, they’ll be forced to stare death in the face. I will reveal which player you have murdered and this will happen in front of everyone,” he explains.
The episode ends with all the players standing in front of the castle, where a giant coffin has been placed inside a circle.
In a teaser for the conclusion to the season, Cumming says in a voiceover, “The final showdown is approaching.”
Who will be the last player or players standing? The answer will be revealed on Thursday, Feb. 26.

title: Eric Dane Dies at 53, Nearly 1 Year After Announcing ALS Diagnosis
summary: Eric Dane has died at 53 after announcing his ALS diagnosis in April 2025, his family confirmed in a statement to TODAY.com.
story: Eric Dane has died at 53, his family confirmed in a statement to TODAY.com.
The actor, known for his work in "Grey's Anatomy" and "Euphoria," announced his ALS diagnosis last year, and died on Feb. 19, 2026.
“With heavy hearts, we share that Eric Dane passed on Thursday afternoon following a courageous battle with ALS," the statement began.

"He spent his final days surrounded by dear friends, his devoted wife, and his two beautiful daughters, Billie and Georgia, who were the center of his world. Throughout his journey with ALS, Eric became a passionate advocate for awareness and research, determined to make a difference for others facing the same fight. He will be deeply missed, and lovingly remembered always. Eric adored his fans and is forever grateful for the outpouring of love and support he’s received. The family has asked for privacy as they navigate this impossible time,” it continued.
ALS is a progressive nervous system disease that affects nerve cells in the brain and spinal cord and causes loss of muscle control, according to the Mayo Clinic.
The disease, which is fatal and has no cure, gradually affects control of the muscles needed to move, speak, eat and breathe.
Dane went public with his ALS diagnosis in a statement to People in April 2025. He also expressed gratitude for his family’s support and said he felt "fortunate" to be able to continue working.
Dane quickly became the public face of the disease, which affects approximately 30,000 people in the United States at any given time, according to the ALS Association.

In September 2025, he set a goal of raising $1 billion toward research for treatments for ALS, and announced on Instagram the he had partnered with “I Am ALS” on its Push for Progress initiative to find a cure for the disease.
Dane and his estranged wife Rebecca Gayheart, who tied the knot in 2004, had remained close friends since their 2018 split, vacationing together with daughters Billie and Georgia.
Once Dane was diagnosed with ALS, Gayheart made it a point to “show up” for the actor, she revealed during a Nov. 17, 2025 appearance on the “Broad Ideas” podcast.

"I am definitely trying to show (our daughters) that we show up for people, no matter what. He is our family. He is your father,” she said. “We show up, and we try to do it with some dignity and some grace and just get through it and that we will get through it the best we can.”
Gayheart also dismissed a petition to divorce she filed in 2018 in March 2025, less than a month before Dane announced his diagnosis.
After going public with his health challenges, Dane went on to star in the Amazon Prime Video crime series “Countdown," which premiered in June 2025.
The same month, he told E! News of his acting career, "I’m going to ride this till the wheels fall off." He added that being on the set of a production was good for his mental health.
"It keeps me sharp. It keeps me moving forward, which is super important right now,” he said.
In November 2025, Dane earned rave reviews for guest starring as a firefighter living with the disease in on an emotional episode of the NBC hospital drama “Brilliant Minds.”

title: 'Love Story': Did John F. Kennedy Jr. Really Break Up With Carolyn Bessette Over an Anonymous Letter?
summary: John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette break up over an anonymous letter in Episode 4 of Love Story. Here's who wrote the letter.
story: John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette's connection grows in Episode 4 of "Love Story." But so do the problems that come with dating the most eligible bachelor in the U.S. and a scion of a political dynasty.

In the episode, things are going well between John (played by Paul Anthony Kelly) and Carolyn (Sarah Pidgeon). Sure, Carolyn's mom is suspicious of their rekindled relationship — but maybe this time, it's different.

Then John finds a letter slipped into his bag after a game of soccer casting doubt on Carolyn. After reading it, everything changes.  John is suddenly suspicious of Carolyn. Is she a user like everyone else?

Doubting her character, John abruptly ends things and Carolyn shuts the door on him just as fast. One apology and a difficult conversation later, though, they have exchanged "I love yous" and are back together.
Elizabeth Beller writes about the real events that inspired this segment in her book "Once Upon a Time," the basis of the show.

According to Beller, the rupture the letter caused was actually more dramatic than what the episode shows. John did receive a letter about Carolyn two months into their love affair, and it led to a "messy" breakup, per Beller.
According to the book, Kennedy and Bessette met at a Tribeca restaurant called El Teddy's a week after they had a blissful weekend away.

"John presented Carolyn with a letter.
